commands.author: SnowBelt
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a464dea48959916a1d437aab7c53d83a6c02550d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Active Memory normalization so omitted `config.agents` targets configured agents or `main`, preserves explicit allowlists, and updates docs, manifest help, and tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +45, Docs +10. Total +61 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows omitted `config.agents` normalizes to `[]`, and both the command and prompt-build gates reject agents outside that list; no live gateway reproduction was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer compatibility/default-policy approval and contributor final-head runtime proof, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: [codex] active-memory default to configured agents
target.body:
## Summary

- Default Active Memory to configured OpenClaw agents when `plugins.entries.active-memory.config.agents` is omitted.
- Fall back to the implicit `main` agent for minimal configs that do not define `agents.list`.
- Preserve explicit empty `config.agents: []` as no targeted agents.
- Preserve explicit non-empty `config.agents` allowlist behavior.
- Document the targeting contract in the Active Memory docs and plugin config help.

This matters because omitting `config.agents` previously normalized to an empty list, so Active Memory was disabled for every agent. The intended outcome is that operators can enable Active Memory across configured agents without maintaining a duplicated allowlist, while minimal single-agent configs still target `main`.

Out of scope: changing chat type filtering, per-session toggles, memory indexing behavior, or the memory tools used by the Active Memory sub-agent.

Review focus: whether the fallback to `agents.list[].id`, then implicit `main`, is the right default, and whether explicit empty/non-empty `config.agents` remains unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #82528

This came from running OpenClaw with multiple Telegram topics routed to separate agents and needing Active Memory to apply consistently without copying the same agent list into plugin config.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed:
  Omitting `plugins.entries.active-memory.config.agents` normalized to an empty allowlist, so Active Memory skipped every agent instead of applying to configured agents.
- Real environment tested:
  OpenClaw 2026.6.5 on a Linux host with a systemd user gateway, Telegram group topics routed to `main`, `sementes`, `cloudfarm`, and `pedro-agenda`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  Applied the equivalent runtime change locally, removed `active-memory.config.agents`, restarted `openclaw-gateway`, then checked gateway status and memory search.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
openclaw gateway status --deep
Gateway version: 2026.6.5
Runtime: running
Capability: admin-capable
```

```text
journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since "3 minutes ago"
[gateway] http server listening (12 plugins: active-memory, brave, browser, canvas, codex, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openai, phone-control, talk-voice, telegram; 12.1s)
```

```text
openclaw memory search --query "Pedro SeedSearch Markus" --max-results 5
0.545 memory/pendencias-grupo.md:1-21
0.516 memory/2026-03-31.md:1-37
0.453 memory/dreaming/light/2026-06-05.md:43-72
```

- Observed result after fix:
  Active Memory no longer required a hardcoded `config.agents` list to apply across configured agents.
- What was not tested:
  A fresh live Telegram command round-trip was not captured on the final PR branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  The production server was restored to upstream behavior after proof collection, using an explicit `config.agents` list until this PR is merged upstream.
- Before evidence:
  Reading the prior plugin implementation showed `normalizePluginConfig({}).agents` became `[]`, while `isEnabledForAgent` requires `config.agents.includes(agentId)`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
pnpm docs:list
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts --maxWorkers=1
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/active-memory/index.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts docs/concepts/active-memory.md extensions/active-memory/openclaw.plugin.json
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/active-memory/index.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/active-memory.md
node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check
node --import tsx -e "import { testing } from './extensions/active-memory/index.ts'; const cfg={agents:{list:[{id:'main'},{id:'support'}]}}; console.log(JSON.stringify({ omitted: testing.normalizePluginConfig({}, cfg).agents, empty: testing.normalizePluginConfig({agents: []}, cfg).agents, explicit: testing.normalizePluginConfig({agents:['support']}, cfg).agents, implicitMain: testing.normalizePluginConfig({}).agents }, null, 2));"
git diff --check upstream/main -- docs/concepts/active-memory.md extensions/active-memory/index.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts extensions/active-memory/openclaw.plugin.json
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD
```

Regression coverage added:

- `defaults to all configured agents when no active-memory agent allowlist is set`
- `preserves an empty active-memory agent allowlist`
- `defaults to the implicit main agent when no agent list is configured`
- `keeps an explicit active-memory agent allowlist`

CI after the latest push:

- Pending for commit `b7e75ed8eaa7`. Prior CI was green on `fdf408d1e55464aced878f0fee85201324590de3`.

Final-head normalization proof on `b7e75ed8eaa7`:

```json
{
  "omitted": [
    "main",
    "support"
  ],
  "empty": [],
  "explicit": [
    "support"
  ],
  "implicitMain": [
    "main"
  ]
}
```

Autoreview note:

- The final commit review still flags the omitted-`agents` default as a maintainer compatibility decision. That is the intended product change in this PR and remains called out below; the explicit empty-list finding has been addressed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

Highest-risk area:
Operators who intentionally omitted `config.agents` expecting Active Memory to run nowhere would now get Active Memory for configured agents, or `main` in minimal configs. Explicit `config.agents: []` continues to target no agents.

Risk mitigation:
Explicit `config.agents: []` remains a no-target allowlist, and explicit non-empty `config.agents` still behaves as an allowlist. Operators can set `enabled: false`, disable the plugin entry, set `agents: []`, or provide a narrow `agents` list when they want less coverage. The docs and manifest help now state the omitted default and the explicit-empty behavior.

## Current review state

Next action:
Await maintainer compatibility decision for omitted `config.agents` and CI completion on the latest commit.

Still waiting on:
GitHub PR checks for the latest push and maintainer decision on the omitted-agent default policy.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed:
Filled in real behavior proof after the `triage: needs-real-behavior-proof` label was applied, then added docs/manifest alignment and focused regression coverage for omitted, explicit-empty, implicit-main, and explicit allowlist targeting. The latest update preserves explicit `agents: []` semantics per ClawSweeper guidance.
target.author: glucksberg

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: Add agent role eval harness
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a minimal agent role evaluation harness: CLI, workflow, workflow helper, reusable contract catalog, tests, and package scripts.
- Creates a clean prerequisite for follow-up Browser Steward runtime-enforcement work without carrying the older 49-commit / 300-file stacked branch.
- Intended outcome: maintainers can review and land the reusable harness independently before Browser Steward adds its focused contract/runtime checks.
- Intentionally out of scope: Browser Steward runtime enforcement, unrelated dashboard/music/backup/SNES work, generated bundle hashes, and any credential/session mutation.
- Review focus: harness boundaries, workflow validity, test coverage, and the fact that the diff is limited to the minimal prerequisite surface.

## Linked context

Related: https://github.com/SnowBelt/openclaw/pull/1
Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93018

Requested by: local OpenClaw agent-build audit workflow to unblock Browser Steward upstream integration safely.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Browser Steward runtime-enforcement PR was blocked by an unsafe prerequisite branch containing 49 commits and 300 files. This PR provides the minimal reusable agent-role-eval harness needed as a clean upstream prerequisite.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/agent-role-eval-harness-clean`, based on current `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm agents:eval:contracts`; `node scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs --contracts-only --json`; `pnpm test test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs scripts/agent-role-eval-workflow.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-evals.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-eval-workflow.mjs test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`; `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`; `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-precommit-venv/bin:$PATH pnpm check:workflows`; `env NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build`.
- Evidence after fix: Local terminal output showed the contract catalog passing with 51 contracts and 8 critical contracts, the focused test file passing 24/24 tests, `zizmor` workflow audit passing, and `pnpm build` completing successfully.
- Observed result after fix: The harness validates deterministically, the workflow passes local workflow sanity, and the build succeeds while the branch remains one commit with seven files changed.
- What was not tested: Scheduled GitHub Actions execution and live agent turns in CI were not run from this PR yet.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:workflows` required a temporary `/tmp` Python virtualenv for `pre-commit`; no repo-tracked dependency files were changed for that local tool setup.
- Before evidence: The previous upstream Browser Steward PR targeted `main` directly and showed 47 commits / 100 files, proving the stacked branch was too large for focused review.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm agents:eval:contracts`
- `node scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs --contracts-only --json`
- `pnpm test test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs scripts/agent-role-eval-workflow.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-evals.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-eval-workflow.mjs test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`
- `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-precommit-venv/bin:$PATH pnpm check:workflows`
- `env NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build`

Regression coverage added:
- `test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts` covers contract catalog validity, live prompt scoring, self-contained eval state, workflow input resolution, live-report verification, Ollama bootstrap planning, configured-agent static checks, and failure cases.

Known validation notes:
- First `pnpm check:workflows` failed because local `pre-commit` was missing; reran successfully using a temporary `/tmp` venv.
- First `pnpm build` failed because sandboxing blocked the repo-local heavy-check lock under Git metadata; reran successfully with escalated local filesystem access.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, adds package scripts and a scheduled/manual workflow.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, adds an evaluation workflow that can run live evals when configured; secrets are read only by the live workflow steps and are not logged by this PR.

Highest-risk area: CI workflow and live-eval secret handling.

Mitigation: Workflow defaults run only deterministic contract catalog checks unless scheduled/live settings opt in; local workflow sanity, `zizmor`, focused tests, and build passed.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and CI on this clean prerequisite PR.

Waiting on: upstream CI and maintainer acceptance. Browser Steward upstream PR should wait until this prerequisite lands or is explicitly accepted.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet on this PR.
target.author: snowbelt
